policy: Canonical ticker universe for conviction ranking. Includes all tracked candidates plus systematically identified coverage gaps. Scores are seed values — conviction-ranking engine applies macro and portfolio modifiers on top.

tickers.name: Taiwan Semiconductor
tickers.whyCore: Manufactures every leading-edge AI chip — NVDA, AVGO, AMD all fab here. Absolute monopoly on 3nm/2nm nodes. No AI hardware stack exists without TSMC.
tickers.moat: Technical monopoly. 3-5 year lead on any competitor. Cannot be replicated.
tickers.riskNote: Taiwan geopolitical risk. But if this risk materializes, entire AI hardware thesis collapses regardless.

tickers.name: AppLovin
tickers.whyCore: AXON 2.0 AI ad targeting model outperforms Google and Meta networks for mobile and e-commerce. Revenue growing 70%+ YoY. Biggest gap in current watchlist — no AI advertising exposure anywhere in portfolio.
tickers.moat: Network effects in ad data. The more advertisers use it, the better the model gets. Classic flywheel.
tickers.riskNote: High valuation. Cyclical ad spend exposure. Regulatory scrutiny of ad tech.

tickers.name: ASML Holding
tickers.whyCore: The only manufacturer of EUV lithography machines. TSMC, Samsung, Intel all buy exclusively from ASML. No leading-edge chip fab operates without it. The chokepoint above the chokepoint.
tickers.moat: Absolute monopoly. 20+ years to replicate. Proprietary technology developed over decades.
tickers.riskNote: Semiconductor capex cycle (equipment orders front-run then slow). China export restrictions reducing TAM.

tickers.name: Vistra Energy
tickers.whyCore: Nuclear + natural gas generation. Direct complement to CEG position. AI data centers need reliable 24/7 baseload power. VST has nuclear assets that cannot be replicated and has historically matched or exceeded CEG returns.
tickers.moat: Nuclear plants are irreplaceable 40-year assets. Cannot build new ones quickly. AI data center demand creates structural pricing power.
tickers.riskNote: Commodity exposure (gas generation). Regulatory environment. More leverage than CEG.

tickers.name: ServiceNow
tickers.whyCore: Enterprise workflow automation platform. >98% revenue retention — once a company runs 500 workflows on NOW, they never leave. AI is accelerating adoption as companies embed LLMs into their workflow automation.
tickers.moat: Extreme switching costs. Workflow processes become deeply embedded in company operations.
tickers.riskNote: High valuation (P/E 55x). Competition from Microsoft Copilot embedded in Office. Rate sensitivity on long-duration growth.

tickers.name: Datadog
tickers.whyCore: Cloud and AI infrastructure observability. Q1 2026: revenue $1.01B (+32% YoY) — first time crossing $1B. FCF $289M at 29% margin. Full-year guide raised by $240M at midpoint to $4.3-4.34B. Four consecutive quarters of accelerating growth. AI workloads generate more observability data, not less — DDOG benefits from AI proliferation.
tickers.moat: Platform stickiness. Observability data is deeply integrated into operations. Net revenue retention >120%.
tickers.riskNote: Software rotation headwind in short term. Cloud providers building native monitoring. Street-high target $320.

tickers.name: Applied Materials
tickers.whyCore: Largest semiconductor equipment company. Makes deposition, etch, and inspection tools for every chip fab. Every new fab (TSMC Arizona, Intel Ohio) orders billions in AMAT equipment. Geopolitical onshoring creates a multi-year supercycle.
tickers.moat: Duopoly with Lam Research. Deep customer integration — switching equipment mid-process is impractical.
tickers.riskNote: Semiconductor capex cycles. China export restrictions. More cyclical than ASML.

tickers.name: Broadcom
tickers.whyCore: Custom ASIC design for hyperscalers + AI networking silicon. Q2 FY2026 (June 3): revenue $22.2B (+48% YoY, beat), AI revenue $10.8B (+143% YoY). Q3 guided $29.4B total, AI revenue >200% YoY. Order book >$70B from Google, Meta, Apple. Stock dipped -3% post-earnings on minor infrastructure software miss — the AI hardware acceleration is confirmed and accelerating.
tickers.moat: Deep hyperscaler relationships. Custom ASIC design is irreplaceable once embedded — hyperscalers design multi-year chip roadmaps around AVGO.
tickers.riskNote: Infrastructure software segment dragging (caused post-earnings dip). Semiconductor cycle exposure if hyperscaler capex decelerates.
tickers.activeSignal: Post-Q2 dip is the entry window. Q3 guided $29.4B (+84% YoY) with AI revenue >200%. Hard data does not support the sell-off narrative.

tickers.name: NVIDIA
tickers.whyCore: Center of the AI accelerator market. H100/H200/B100 GPU demand remains backlogged. Data center revenue growing 400%+ YoY. The consensus pick — but the fundamentals justify it.
tickers.moat: CUDA software ecosystem. 10+ year developer lock-in. No competitor has matched the full hardware + software stack.
tickers.riskNote: Export controls risk. Valuation premium requires sustained AI capex. Consensus = less asymmetry.

tickers.name: Eaton
tickers.whyCore: Electrical components, power management, data center power infrastructure. Less visible than nuclear plays but more durable — every data center, every EV charger, every grid upgrade uses Eaton components.
tickers.moat: Industrial switching costs. Long-term utility and data center supply relationships.
tickers.riskNote: Industrial cyclicality. Premium valuation for the sector.

tickers.name: Palantir
tickers.whyCore: Government AI deployment moat. AIP platform adopted by US military and intelligence. Q1 2026 revenue +85% YoY, FCF $925M at 57% margin. Business is genuinely exceptional — the problem is valuation.
tickers.moat: Government contract stickiness. Security clearance requirements create near-impossible switching.
tickers.riskNote: P/S ~80x trailing. P/E 215x+. CEO Karp sold $65.9M open-market Feb 2026. Jefferies $70 target (vs ~$132). 40% revenue from US government — sequestration risk. International commercial grew only 2% FY2025. Does NOT pass capital preservation filter.

tickers.name: Nextpower
tickers.whyCore: Solar tracker hardware and software for utility-scale solar. Rebranded from Nextracker to Nextpower in Nov 2025 to reflect evolution into integrated power technology platform. FY2026 revenue $3.56B (+20% YoY), FCF $514M, record backlog $5.25B.
tickers.moat: Software-enabled hardware — tracking optimization creates switching costs.
tickers.riskNote: Solar cyclicality. Tariff impact on gross margins (recovering). Policy risk under current administration.
tickers.activeSignal: Record $5.25B backlog with tariff headwind absorbing — gross margin recovery trajectory is the key proof point.

tickers.name: Vertiv
tickers.whyCore: Data center power and cooling infrastructure. Every AI GPU cluster generates massive heat. Vertiv makes the cooling systems that keep them running.
tickers.moat: Deep data center integration — cooling systems are designed into facilities, not swapped.
tickers.riskNote: Valuation elevated. Theme crowding. Industrial cyclicality.

tickers.name: Alphabet
tickers.whyCore: Search monopoly + YouTube + Google Cloud (GCP) + DeepMind AI. Diversified quality compounder with AI optionality. Trading at a discount to Microsoft and Meta on forward earnings.
tickers.moat: Search network effects + advertising data flywheel + YouTube creator ecosystem.
tickers.riskNote: Antitrust/regulatory pressure. Search disruption narrative from AI. AI capex margin drag.

tickers.name: Cameco
tickers.whyCore: Largest publicly traded uranium producer. Nuclear fuel supply chain exposure as reactor demand grows from data center electricity needs.
tickers.moat: Tier-1 uranium mines with 20+ year reserves. Permitting for new mines takes decades.
tickers.riskNote: Commodity cycle risk. Geopolitical mine risk (Kazakhstan). Narrative crowding.
tickers.activeSignal: Down -8.61% — sharp sector dislocation alongside other nuclear names.

tickers.name: GE Vernova
tickers.whyCore: Gas turbines, wind, grid modernization equipment. Direct beneficiary of grid capacity shortage as AI data centers require more electricity.
tickers.moat: Long-term service agreements on turbines. Fleet installed base creates recurring revenue.
tickers.riskNote: Industrial cyclicality. Execution on margin improvement. Theme crowding after power narrative enthusiasm.

tickers.name: Quanta Services
tickers.whyCore: Physical grid construction — high-voltage transmission lines, substations, data center electrical work. Every grid upgrade, every new data center, every renewable farm needs Quanta's crews.
tickers.moat: Skilled labor scarcity creates barrier to entry. Long-term utility framework agreements.
tickers.riskNote: Project execution. Utility capex timing. Rate-sensitive valuation.

tickers.name: Reddit
tickers.whyCore: Human-generated conversation data as scarce AI training input. Data licensing deals with Google and others. Ad monetization improving.
tickers.moat: Community network effects. Authentic human discourse increasingly scarce for AI training.
tickers.riskNote: Post-IPO volatility. Platform governance risk. Ad-cycle sensitivity.

tickers.name: TransMedics
tickers.whyCore: Organ transplant preservation and logistics network. OCS (Organ Care System) dramatically expands usable donor organ pool. Network effects as more transplant centers use the platform.
tickers.moat: Transplant center relationships + regulatory approvals + network effects.
tickers.riskNote: Execution volatility. Healthcare reimbursement risk. Small-cap valuation compression.
